Short_Description: Measure area for project planning, detailed work defined.

Detailed Description:
General Notes for all Excavation Items: Contractor shall take all required permissions from the appropriate  authorities for Excavation, Blasting, Carting away materials etc. and shall bear all the expenses for the same.
Rate shall include 
1. Removal of all kinds of vegetation, small shrubs etc. If there are any shrubs, bushes, roots of the trees to be removed for min depth 1.5m including all necessary permissions. Lift & lead outside campus are to be considered to suit the local regulations.  The necessary approval shall be taken for removal of trees with the consent of the clients if applicable. The contractor shall be fully responsible for liasoning with local authoritites, compliance to all the statutory obligations, including all labour lanes in force, facilities, policies etc. towards all employees , labour and under no circumstance nor any acting party pass on the responsiblitites of the contractor  to the other contractor/clients.
2. Contractor shall get acquainted with site conditions, nature of soil and adopt suitable dewatering system as deemed fit for the nature of soil and prevailing water table and water collected by runoff or rain needs to be disposed off from the site.  The rate shall include keeping the excavated surface dry till completion of the scope of work. Dewatered water/ Slush  shall be drained out by suitable means to places not objected by civic authorities. The contractor shall submit the method statement for dewatering and the same shall be let out of the building premises through proper pipes & channels as required.
3. The rate shall include necessary general shoring, strutting with approved timber/steel accessories wherever necessary as approved by the Client / the Engineer.
4. Contour level to be taken jointly with the Project Manager and prior written approval be taken before starting any excavation/ filling at every 5 mt grids for arriving at the actual quantity using simpsons rule.  Cost to include all and necessary royalty and other permission charges required to execute the works.
5. The rate shall include all necessary setting out of building, excavation line, barrication by grid/plot marking, survey by Total station, transfer of bench mark, etc., complete
6.The quoted rates includes seggregating of good quality excavated earth from the clay / silty soil and storing in designted area for further treatment within the site premises for future landscape purposes including preserving top 300 mm layer of existing soil to meet MOEF requirements.. No extra is payable towards double handling.
7. Quoted rates shall incldue the preparation of temporary access road / Pathways, cross over, foot path, tracks, hard standings, temporary ramp to approach the excavated level etc, and temporary drains, rerouting of existing roads, supporting systems for existing MEP services if any and re-routing if required, etc. complete. at no extra cost.
8.  Carting away all types of excavated earth outside or within the site shall be included and environmental norms to be followed for discarding excavated material with submission of document of proof.
9. Soil shall be classified as three types for billing purpose.
a.Classification under hard rock  excavation item which requires chiselling with compressed air/mechanical equipment's,controlled blasting, chemical splitting, diamond cutting etc. with necessary mechanical equipment's and loose boulders which requires splitting and handling.
b. Classification under soft/medium/ weathered rock  excavation item which is non poclainable, can be excavated using rock breaker attachments to JCB/Poclain, manual chiselling, etc. and loose boulders removal without spliting etc.
c. Classification under soil  excavation which is poclainable, can be excavated using JCB/Poclain etc.
10. Please note that the water and power required for construction shall be arranged by contractor and shall be included in the rates quoted. Necessary bore wells , bringing water from tankers from outside, DG, temporary power, power distribution lines etc are all part of the rates quoted.
11. The method statement shall include all safety features and protection to workmen, third party as well as property and services nearby. Methods of shoring and strutting should be fully detailed in the method statement. Removal of excavated material from excavated area, method of handling, method of storage (including any handling if necessary) as well as disposal shall be explained fully. Where controlled blasting is restricted, the contractor should ensure that all necessary statutory approvals are obtained in advance and kept in current and all necessary insurance and other covers shall be taken and kept valid in the case of sub-contracting this job, such cover shall include sub-contractor's workman also. Where controlled blasting is resorted to, contractor should take all necessary precaution for ensuring safety of life end property in the vicinity.
12. Carting away of excavated soil/surplus /dense soil/ disintegrated/ weathered/ soft rock /other materials away from the site to a place designated and not objected by civic authorities by mechanical means including lead, lift, loading, unloading etc., complete. Including taking necessary approval from local authorities, environmental norms to be followed for discarding earth with submission of document of proof and fee if any. For the purpose of payment, quantities from the excavated area based on contour profile shall be accounted and loose earth or truck load will not be measured and paid. Rate to include all royalties, Seignorage, taking statutory approvals, traffic permits from Police and Local body, as necessary to carry out the works etc., complete. Disposal should also to an approved site only.  No Lift / lead distance or limitation is specified or accepted. Contractor are required to examine the scope of work, inspect the site and find out before quoting the approved disposal points. No increase in rate is allowable on account of difference between lift / lead distances.
13. The rates quoted shall include for Excavation either straight, inclined or curved in basements, etc., including marking out, cutting and dressing sides and bottoms to true dimensions and deposition of excavated material as described.
14. Qty. shall be arrived at after taking block levels taken prior to and after excavation. The below mentioned quantities are based on soil report and the quantities may vary as per site condition. Quantity  mentioned is tentative and may vary to any extent depending upon underlying ground profile to be exposed during excavation.
15.Trimming all sides as instructed at site, levelling all bottoms, clearing out all loose earth etc. The rates to include necessary cutting, trimming and filling with compaction .

Note: The percentage of voids to be deducted from stack measurement is 40% for hardrock or Initial levels will be taken before blasting hard rock and final levels will be taken after achieving required levels in presence of PIC. Note: The percentage of voids to be deducted from stack measurement is 40% for hardrock or Initial levels will be taken before blasting hard rock and final levels will be taken after achieving required levels in presence of PIC. Note: PCC area shall be considered for measurement (Working space & provision of slopes if required for execution shall not be paid separately. Mode of measurement shall be by joint measurements/ levels before and after excavation)., With excavated earth available at site, With Brought outside earth, Providing Pre-constructional anti- termite treatment and creating a chemical barrier to the building by injecting chemical emulsion of required concentrations under grade slab and Grade beam, around the Plinth, Plinth filling, trenches, junction of wall and floor, Retaining / side walls of basement floor, external perimeter of building, surrounding of pipes, expansion joints, below UG sump and .as per instructions of chemical manufacturer and all . The treatment shall be carried out strictly in accordance with the technical specification and conforming to IS 6313 or equivalent BS specification. The chemical to be used as insecticides for the treatment shall be 50% EC (Imidacloprid 30.5 SC) and the application shall be dilute one part of chemical 50% EC with 49 parts of water to get 1% emulsion and strictly in accordance with the manufacturer's specification. Note : Measurement taken along external periphery of the building. Treatment shall extend to a width of 1m around the building. ( 10 years warranty).

Excavation in all types of soil including dense soil, laterite reddish brown/Laterite reddish brown with patches/ dense soil disintegrated/ weathered/ soft rock (Poclainable) by mechanical means including manual excavation for levelling, dressing the edges, with all leads and lifts. Dewatering of both ground and surface water, removal of slurry generated while excavation and keeping the area free of water with necessary shoring, strutting, required for keeping earth in position etc, 
Note: PCC area shall be considered for measurement (Working space & provision of slopes if required for execution shall not be paid separately).

Specification & scope of work same as item No: 1.02.a but for Excavation in Hard rock / boulders (more than 1/2 Cum in size -Un poclainable) by chiselling/ wedging to required level to the required depth including all leads and lifts, dressing, grading, cleaning, excavating loose pockets directed and disposing etc. as per detailed specification and conforming to safety rules of Authority during the progress of work.  
Note: The percentage of voids to be deducted from stack measurement is 40% for hardrock or Initial levels will be taken before blasting hard rock and final levels will be taken after achieving required levels in presence of PIC.
Note: PCC area shall be considered for measurement (Working space & provision of slopes if required for execution shall not be paid separately. Mode of measurement shall be by joint measurements/ levels before and after excavation).

Specification & scope of work same as item No: 1.02.a but for Excavation in Hard rock / boulders (more than 1/2 Cum in size -Un poclainable) by Diamond Rope Cutting to required level to the required depth including all leads and lifts, dressing, grading, cleaning, excavating loose pockets directed and disposing etc. as per detailed specification and conforming to safety rules of Authority during the progress of work.  
Note: The percentage of voids to be deducted from stack measurement is 40% for hardrock or Initial levels will be taken before blasting hard rock and final levels will be taken after achieving required levels in presence of PIC.
Note: PCC area shall be considered for measurement (Working space & provision of slopes if required for execution shall not be paid separately. Mode of measurement shall be by joint measurements/ levels before and after excavation).

Detailed Description: Supply and filling in foundations and the area whereever specified with approved good quality filling materials in plinths, area development etc. wherever specified  in layers of not exceeding 150 mm thick including breaking clods,storing,transportation, double handling, watering, compacting each layer with vibratory compactor and at unaccessible places with wooden/steel rammers to achieve 90 to 95% proctor density at optimum moisture content, all leads and lifts, bailing/ pumping out of water to keep site dry while backfilling; cost shall include conveyance of all materials, labour, machinery etc. complete as directed.The rate to includes loading,unloading, hire and fuel charges for tools and plants and other incidental charges etc., complete. Cost to include all necessary royalty and other permission charges required to execute the works.
LLM Text:
TYPE: SUBITEM

PARENT ITEM DESCRIPTION:
General Notes for all Excavation Items: Contractor shall take all required permissions from the appropriate authorities for Excavation, Blasting, Carting away . and shall bear all the expenses for the same. Rate shall include 1. Removal of all kinds of vegetation, small shrubs . If there are any shrubs, bushes, roots of the trees to be removed for min depth 1.5m all necessary permissions. Lift & lead outside campus are to be considered to suit the local regulations. The necessary approval shall be taken for removal of trees with the consent of the clients if applicable. The contractor shall be fully responsible for liasoning with local authoritites, compliance to all the statutory obligations, all lanes in force, facilities, policies . towards all employees , and under no circumstance nor any acting party pass on the responsiblitites of the contractor to the other contractor/clients. 2. Contractor shall get acquainted with site conditions, nature of soil and adopt suitable dewatering system as deemed fit for the nature of soil and prevailing water table and water collected by runoff or rain needs to be disposed off from the site. The rate shall include keeping the excavated surface dry till completion of the scope of work. Dewatered water/ Slush shall be drained out by suitable means to places not objected by civic authorities. The contractor shall submit the method statement for dewatering and the same shall be let out of the building premises through proper pipes & channels as required. 3. The rate shall include necessary general shoring, strutting with approved timber/steel accessories wherever necessary as approved by the Client / the Engineer. 4. Contour level to be taken jointly with the Project Manager and prior written approval be taken before starting any excavation/ filling at every 5 mt grids for arriving at the actual quantity using simpsons rule. Cost to include all and necessary royalty and other permission charges required to execute the works. 5. The rate shall include all necessary setting out of building, excavation line, barrication by grid/plot marking, survey by Total station, transfer of bench mark, ., 6.The quoted rates includes seggregating of good quality excavated earth from the clay / silty soil and storing in designted area for further treatment within the site premises for future landscape purposes preserving top 300 mm layer of existing soil to meet MOEF requirements.. No extra is payable towards double handling. 7. Quoted rates shall incldue the preparation of temporary access road / Pathways, cross over, foot path, tracks, hard standings, temporary ramp to approach the excavated level , and temporary drains, rerouting of existing roads, supporting systems for existing MEP services if any and re-routing if required, . . at no extra cost. 8. Carting away all types of excavated earth outside or within the site shall be included and environmental norms to be followed for discarding excavated material with submission of document of proof. 9. Soil shall be classified as three types for billing purpose. a.Classification under hard rock excavation item which requires chiselling with compressed air/mechanical equipment's,controlled blasting, chemical splitting, diamond cutting . with necessary mechanical equipment's and loose boulders which requires splitting and handling. b. Classification under soft/medium/ weathered rock excavation item which is non poclainable, can be excavated using rock breaker attachments to JCB/Poclain, manual chiselling, . and loose boulders removal without spliting . c. Classification under soil excavation which is poclainable, can be excavated using JCB/Poclain . 10. Please note that the water and power required for construction shall be arranged by contractor and shall be included in the rates quoted. Necessary bore wells , bringing water from tankers from outside, DG, temporary power, power distribution lines are all part of the rates quoted. 11. The method statement shall include all safety features and protection to workmen, third party as well as property and services nearby. Methods of shoring and strutting should be fully detailed in the method statement. Removal of excavated material from excavated area, method of handling, method of storage ( any handling if necessary) as well as disposal shall be explained fully. Where controlled blasting is restricted, the contractor should ensure that all necessary statutory approvals are obtained in advance and kept in current and all necessary insurance and other covers shall be taken and kept valid in the case of sub-contracting this job, such cover shall include sub-contractor's workman also. Where controlled blasting is resorted to, contractor should take all necessary precaution for ensuring safety of life end property in the vicinity. 12. Carting away of excavated soil/surplus /dense soil/ disintegrated/ weathered/ soft rock /other away from the site to a place designated and not objected by civic authorities by mechanical means lead, lift, loading, unloading ., . taking necessary approval from local authorities, environmental norms to be followed for discarding earth with submission of document of proof and fee if any. For the purpose of payment, quantities from the excavated area based on contour profile shall be accounted and loose earth or truck load will not be measured and paid. Rate to include all royalties, Seignorage, taking statutory approvals, traffic permits from Police and Local body, as necessary to carry out the works ., . Disposal should also to an approved site only. No Lift / lead distance or limitation is specified or accepted. Contractor are required to examine the scope of work, inspect the site and find out before quoting the approved disposal points. No increase in rate is allowable on account of difference between lift / lead distances. 13. The rates quoted shall include for Excavation either straight, inclined or curved in basements, ., marking out, cutting and dressing sides and bottoms to true dimensions and deposition of excavated material as described. 14. Qty. shall be arrived at after taking block levels taken prior to and after excavation. The below mentioned quantities are based on soil report and the quantities may vary as per site condition. Quantity mentioned is tentative and may vary to any extent depending upon underlying ground profile to be exposed during excavation. 15.Trimming all sides as instructed at site, levelling all bottoms, clearing out all loose earth . The rates to include necessary cutting, trimming and filling with compaction .

Cost to include all necessary royalty and other permission charges required to execute the works.

Providing Pre-constructional anti- termite treatment and creating a chemical barrier to the building by injecting chemical emulsion of required concentrations under grade slab and Grade beam, around the Plinth, Plinth filling, trenches, junction of wall and floor, Retaining / side walls of basement floor, external perimeter of building, surrounding of pipes, expansion joints, below UG sump and .as per instructions of chemical manufacturer and all . Concrete work, detailed specifications, and construction.
Detailed Description:
Unless otherwise specified rates quoted for all PCC works shall include the following :
Cost of all materials, labour, equipment / tools & plants required, lead and lifts, conveyance, infrastructure facilities required etc.
The contractors are particularly advised to study the relevant drawings carefully before quoting.
Minimum cementitious content for concrete shall be as per cement constants specified in the technical specification.
Cost of suitable form work as per site requirement.
Adequate protection to edges and corners of Plain Cement Concrete works from damage during construction.
Keeping the work, well watered for 3-5 days for plain cement concrete works  after casting and it shall confirm to IS Specification.
Coarse graded sand/ manufactured sand of approved make shall be used for all concrete works.
Machine crushed coarse graded aggregate shall be hard broken blue granite of sizes specified.
Cement for all concrete works shall only be High strength ordinary portland cement Grade-53.
Minimum cementitious content for specified design mix shall be in accordance with specification. Minimum cementitious content will be the same as specified in the Technical specification Irrespective of grade of cement used. (Design mix shall be as approved by the Structural Consultant. GGBS can be considered up to 50% of the overall cementious content in concrete mix design for structural elements.)
Providing and laying plain cement concrete at any heights and levels including the cost of all materials , labour, leads and lifts, tools and plant, machinery as per specification with necessary shuttering etc complete. In the concreting works if water table is encountered or water collected by surface runoff, then the rate for concreting should include the cost of dewatering up to the final set of cement. The concrete shall be produced at site using batching plant / weigh batcher at site including any pumping if required. The fine aggregates shall be manufactured sand.
All plain cement concrete works shall be done in accordance with technical specification.

Detailed Description:
Unless otherwise specified rates quoted for all RCC works shall include the following:
Cost of all materials, labour, equipment / tools & plants required, conveyance, infrastructure facilities required etc.
All RCC items include batch weighing of cement and aggregates.
For all RCC works cement concrete shall be of approved design mix.
All concrete used for reinforced concrete work (structural concrete) shall be "Controlled concrete". Contractors shall submit drawings showing the scheme of form work system adopted (shuttering) for all structural elements at various levels as indicated in drawings and this shall be obligatory. 'The Contractor shall maintain at site, cube testing machine in good working order, sieves, slump cone, graduated cylinder and weights, verticality laser machine, scales and other equipment's for material testing.
Form work (which shall be measured and paid separately under relevant item) include splayed edges, notching, allowances for overlaps and passing at angles, battens, strutting, bolting, wedging, casing, rocking, centring, boxing, shuttering, propping strut braces etc., removal thereof and dressing the shuttering with oil to prevent, cement sticking to shutter faces. Form work shall be either straight, curved, plain or tapered and also with provision for specified slopes, if directed. Wind tie bolts for wall shuttering.
Keeping the work, well watered for 10 days after casting and it shall confirm to IS Specification.
Placing in position any inserts like bolts, plates, sleeves, plugs for fixing window frames in RCC members, pipes etc.
The contractors are particularly advised to study the relevant drawings carefully before quoting.
Adequate protection to corners and sides from damage during the construction.
Consolidation using vibrators of approved type as directed.
Work on all floors at levels as per drawing.
Reinforcement steel is measured and paid under relevant item separately.
Before concreting moisture content in the aggregates shall be found out and necessary corrections shall be applied as per Mix Design report.
Rate to include bonding agents for bonding old concrete to new concrete wherever necessary.
All Construction joints shall be at pre approved locations only. All Construction joints and concrete set surface shall be chipped immediately after final setting using mechanical chippers and thoroughly cleaned and shall be treated with epoxy bonding agent while concreting further without any extra cost. Expanded GI mesh shall be considered between the junction of difference in grade of concrete only in Column Drop, column drop & slab, column/core wall  with shear walls.
Roughening and hacking the concrete surfaces wherever required after removal of centring or shuttering. All tie rod holes shall be filled with approved grouting material.
Forming key and construction joints, providing cement slurry work and cleaning at any stage of work, sand papering of existing reinforcement bars of column and dowels etc. as directed before taking up fresh work.
Coarse graded sand/ manufactured sand shall be used for all concrete works.
Machine crushed coarse graded aggregate shall be hard broken blue granite of sizes specified.
Cement for all concrete works shall only be High strength ordinary Portland cement Grade-53.
Designing the concrete mix and batching as per specifications and approved concrete design mix with necessary admixtures for various workability and surface finish requirements.
Adding of admixture to meet the required standards and specifications for various workability, consistency, self compaction, retardation, acceleration, surface finish, colour, uniformity, strength, shrinkage control, durability, fire rating, cohesiveness in the concrete as appropriate.
Limiting the temperature of concrete and heat of hydration by adopting various temperature control measures like usage ice water, soaking of aggregates in water, storage in shaded areas, transportation and pumping with protective covers etc to meet the requirements. The rate shall include use of plasticizers and retarders to limit the water/cement ratio to 0.5 and the temperature to 35 degree C at the time of placing. Complete care shall be taken to achieve dense and fully watertight concrete.
Providing sunk floors, pockets, cut-outs, holes etc.
Taking test cubes for all concrete items and  testing them as specified in IS 456 at the site laboratory to be set up with proper equipment etc. and other regular test shall be carried out as directed either at site Laboratory or at any approved test laboratory.
Cost of in situ test on concrete like slump test, L-Box test, U box test, core test, ultrasonic test, rebound hammer test etc to prove the soundness of the element is included in rates and shall be carried out even though cube test results are passing the strength criteria as required by the consultants.
Cost to include appropriate dewatering wherever necessary, cost of all materials, labours, all lead and lifts, tools, plant and machinery, hire and fuel charges and all other incidental charges etc., all as per specifications, drawings and as directed.
For all structural concrete elements post construction dimension and levels shall be documented and submitted.
Concrete pour plan with construction joints, pour joints, shrinkage strips shall be submitted for approval. Shuttering below the shrinkage strip / pour joints at every level shall be retained in place without disturbing for a time as specified by the structural engineer. Any rectification of  honeycombs not affecting structural stability, slurry leakage shall be rectified as per the methodology duly approved by client / the Engineer without any extra cost.
All the material test certificates from the manufacturer and the external approved reputed testing laboratory, standard deviation and post concreting check results shall be submitted to the consultant.
(Design mix shall be as approved by the Structural Consultant / Employers representative. Cementacious material GGBS can be used in the design mix with a proportion of 65:35 for grade concrete upto M35. GGBS 25% for grade concrete M40 and above. Raft concrete can be 50% of GGBS. Polycarboxylic Ether based admixures to be used for the Concrete of higher grade than M40 and Micro silica shall be used for the concrete of grade higher than M45)
For Terrace Slab / Beam - No GGBS/Fly ash is allowed
Manufactured sand can be considered .
Core test shall be conducted on all elements for acceptance of strength of concrete achieved at site irrespective of the cube strength achieved as confirmatory test for the stability of the building. The total no. of core test shall generally be restricted to 50 Nos of the total concrete volume.
Making all sunk floors, pockets, cutouts, holes, sleeves, embedding electrical conduits, junction boxes, fan hooks, cutout boxes and other inserts, sleeves hold fasts, pipes  etc as specified in the drawings.
All the lifting equipments including tower crane, boom placer, Pumps, pumping lines, material hoist, lifts shall have independent supporting systems and shall not transfer any stress on building components.
Minimum cementitious content for concrete shall be as per cement constants enclosed in the Technical specification.
All Reinforced cement concrete works shall be done in accordance with technical specification .
Providing & laying plant mixed design mix reinforced cement concrete conforming to grades as specified in (IS 456 - 2000) and as per specifications mentioned below at all levels using 20mm & down size aggregates, fine aggregates, OPC, necessary admixtures approved by Consultants (Conplast SP 430SRV from Fosroc or equivalent), including designing the mix, trial cubes, accelerated curing, weigh batching, mechanical mixing, transporting, pumping and placing at all levels, excluding the cost of steel reinforcement, centring, shuttering but inclusive of cost towards pumping using line pump or boom placer, scaffolding, compacting with vibrators, construction joint treatments, curing and hacking the exposed surfaces wherever required to receive plaster or floated steel trowelled surface whenever specified. The rate shall include cost of  arrangements to provide recesses to slabs, columns, walls and placing and securing properly within the formwork sleeves and / or opening in slabs, construction joints, laying to required shape, size and slope all as per drawings. 
The mix proportion of cement with that of GGBS shall be  confirming to latest IS standards.
The rate shall include providing and fixing the required PVC sleeves up to 200 mm dia pockets and opening etc., cleaning the site debris stacking the material, hoisting equipment's, shutter accessories and fixing pipe / plates / inserts in perfect size level , plumb etc. as directed by Engineer In Charge and as per technical specifications in the tender. Repairing / replacing the formwork, filling all the holes created due to shuttering / sleeves provided for passage of plumbing, FF pipes / Electrical cables with approved non shrink filler material etc. complete to the satisfaction of Client / the Engineer. Hacking of RCC faces as specified and directed by Engineer In Charge to be included in quoted rate of RCC.
Cost to include appropriate dewatering wherever necessary, cost of all materials, labours, all lead and lifts, tools, plant and machinery, hire and fuel charges and all other incidental charges etc., all as per specifications, drawings and as directed.

TYPE: ITEM

ITEM DESCRIPTION:
Unless otherwise specified rates quoted for all RCC works shall include the following: Cost of all , , equipment / tools & plants required, conveyance, infrastructure facilities required . All RCC items include batch weighing of cement and aggregates. For all RCC works cement concrete shall be of approved design mix. All concrete used for reinforced concrete work (structural concrete) shall be "Controlled concrete". Contractors shall submit drawings showing the scheme of form work system adopted (shuttering) for all structural elements at various levels as indicated in drawings and this shall be obligatory. 'The Contractor shall maintain at site, cube testing machine in good working order, sieves, slump cone, graduated cylinder and weights, verticality laser machine, scales and other equipment's for material testing. Form work (which shall be measured and paid separately under relevant item) include splayed edges, notching, allowances for overlaps and passing at angles, battens, strutting, bolting, wedging, casing, rocking, centring, boxing, shuttering, propping strut braces ., removal thereof and dressing the shuttering with oil to prevent, cement sticking to shutter faces. Form work shall be either straight, curved, plain or tapered and also with provision for specified slopes, if directed. Wind tie bolts for wall shuttering. Keeping the work, well watered for 10 days after casting and it shall confirm to IS Specification. Placing in position any inserts like bolts, plates, sleeves, plugs for fixing window frames in RCC members, pipes . The contractors are particularly advised to study the relevant drawings carefully before quoting. Adequate protection to corners and sides from damage during the construction. Consolidation using vibrators of approved type . Work on all floors at levels as per drawing. Reinforcement steel is measured and paid under relevant item separately. Before concreting moisture content in the aggregates shall be found out and necessary corrections shall be applied as per Mix Design report. Rate to include bonding agents for bonding old concrete to new concrete wherever necessary. All Construction joints shall be at pre approved locations only. All Construction joints and concrete set surface shall be chipped immediately after final setting using mechanical chippers and thoroughly cleaned and shall be treated with epoxy bonding agent while concreting further without any extra cost. Expanded GI mesh shall be considered between the junction of difference in grade of concrete only in Column Drop, column drop & slab, column/core wall with shear walls. Roughening and hacking the concrete surfaces wherever required after removal of centring or shuttering. All tie rod holes shall be filled with approved grouting material. Forming key and construction joints, providing cement slurry work and cleaning at any stage of work, sand papering of existing reinforcement bars of column and dowels . before taking up fresh work. Coarse graded sand/ manufactured sand shall be used for all concrete works. Machine crushed coarse graded aggregate shall be hard broken blue granite of sizes specified. Cement for all concrete works shall only be High strength ordinary Portland cement Grade-53. Designing the concrete mix and batching s and approved concrete design mix with necessary admixtures for various workability and surface finish requirements. Adding of admixture to meet the required standards and specifications for various workability, consistency, self compaction, retardation, acceleration, surface finish, colour, uniformity, strength, shrinkage control, durability, fire rating, cohesiveness in the concrete as appropriate. Limiting the temperature of concrete and heat of hydration by adopting various temperature control measures like usage ice water, soaking of aggregates in water, storage in shaded areas, transportation and pumping with protective covers to meet the requirements. The rate shall include use of plasticizers and retarders to limit the water/cement ratio to 0.5 and the temperature to 35 degree C at the time of placing. care shall be taken to achieve dense and fully watertight concrete. Providing sunk floors, pockets, cut-outs, holes . Taking test cubes for all concrete items and testing them as specified in IS 456 at the site laboratory to be set up with proper equipment . and other regular test shall be carried out either at site Laboratory or at any approved test laboratory. Cost of in situ test on concrete like slump test, L-Box test, U box test, core test, ultrasonic test, rebound hammer test to prove the soundness of the element is included in rates and shall be carried out even though cube test results are passing the strength criteria as required by the consultants. Cost to include appropriate dewatering wherever necessary, cost of all , s, all lead and lifts, tools, plant and machinery, hire and fuel charges and all other incidental charges ., all s, drawings and . For all structural concrete elements post construction dimension and levels shall be documented and submitted. Concrete pour plan with construction joints, pour joints, shrinkage strips shall be submitted for approval. Shuttering below the shrinkage strip / pour joints at every level shall be retained in place without disturbing for a time as specified by the structural engineer. Any rectification of honeycombs not affecting structural stability, slurry leakage shall be rectified as per the methodology duly approved by client / the Engineer without any extra cost. All the material test certificates from the manufacturer and the external approved reputed testing laboratory, standard deviation and post concreting check results shall be submitted to the consultant. (Design mix shall be as approved by the Structural Consultant / Employers representative. Cementacious material GGBS can be used in the design mix with a proportion of 65:35 for grade concrete upto M35. GGBS 25% for grade concrete M40 and above. Raft concrete can be 50% of GGBS. Polycarboxylic Ether based admixures to be used for the Concrete of higher grade than M40 and Micro silica shall be used for the concrete of grade higher than M45) For Terrace Slab / Beam - No GGBS/Fly ash is allowed Manufactured sand can be considered . Core test shall be conducted on all elements for acceptance of strength of concrete achieved at site irrespective of the cube strength achieved as confirmatory test for the stability of the building. The total no. of core test shall generally be restricted to 50 Nos of the total concrete volume. Making all sunk floors, pockets, cutouts, holes, sleeves, embedding electrical conduits, junction boxes, fan hooks, cutout boxes and other inserts, sleeves hold fasts, pipes as specified in the drawings. All the lifting equipments tower crane, boom placer, Pumps, pumping lines, material hoist, lifts shall have independent supporting systems and shall not transfer any stress on building components. Minimum cementitious content for concrete shall be as per cement constants enclosed in the Technical specification. All Reinforced cement concrete works shall be done in accordance with technical specification . Providing & laying plant mixed design mix reinforced cement concrete conforming to grades as specified in (IS 456 - 2000) and s mentioned below at all levels using 20mm & down size aggregates, fine aggregates, OPC, necessary admixtures approved by Consultants (Conplast SP 430SRV from Fosroc or equivalent), designing the mix, trial cubes, accelerated curing, weigh batching, mechanical mixing, transporting, pumping and placing at all levels, excluding the cost of steel reinforcement, centring, shuttering but inclusive of cost towards pumping using line pump or boom placer, scaffolding, compacting with vibrators, construction joint treatments, curing and hacking the exposed surfaces wherever required to receive plaster or floated steel trowelled surface whenever specified. The rate shall include cost of arrangements to provide recesses to slabs, columns, walls and placing and securing properly within the formwork sleeves and / or opening in slabs, construction joints, laying to required shape, size and slope all as per drawings. The mix proportion of cement with that of GGBS shall be confirming to latest IS standards. The rate shall include providing and fixing the required PVC sleeves up to 200 mm dia pockets and opening ., cleaning the site debris stacking the material, hoisting equipment's, shutter accessories and fixing pipe / plates / inserts in perfect size level , plumb . by Engineer In Charge and as per technical specifications in the tender. Repairing / replacing the formwork, filling all the holes created due to shuttering / sleeves provided for passage of plumbing, FF pipes / Electrical cables with approved non shrink filler material . to the satisfaction of Client / the Engineer. Hacking of RCC faces as specified and directed by Engineer In Charge to be included in quoted rate of RCC. Cost to include appropriate dewatering wherever necessary, cost of all , s, all lead and lifts, tools, plant and machinery, hire and fuel charges and all other incidental charges ., all s, drawings and .

PARENT ITEM DESCRIPTION:
Unless otherwise specified rates quoted for all RCC works shall include the following: Cost of all , , equipment / tools & plants required, conveyance, infrastructure facilities required . All RCC items include batch weighing of cement and aggregates. For all RCC works cement concrete shall be of approved design mix. All concrete used for reinforced concrete work (structural concrete) shall be "Controlled concrete". Contractors shall submit drawings showing the scheme of form work system adopted (shuttering) for all structural elements at various levels as indicated in drawings and this shall be obligatory. 'The Contractor shall maintain at site, cube testing machine in good working order, sieves, slump cone, graduated cylinder and weights, verticality laser machine, scales and other equipment's for material testing. Form work (which shall be measured and paid separately under relevant item) include splayed edges, notching, allowances for overlaps and passing at angles, battens, strutting, bolting, wedging, casing, rocking, centring, boxing, shuttering, propping strut braces ., removal thereof and dressing the shuttering with oil to prevent, cement sticking to shutter faces. Form work shall be either straight, curved, plain or tapered and also with provision for specified slopes, if directed. Wind tie bolts for wall shuttering. Keeping the work, well watered for 10 days after casting and it shall confirm to IS Specification. Placing in position any inserts like bolts, plates, sleeves, plugs for fixing window frames in RCC members, pipes . The contractors are particularly advised to study the relevant drawings carefully before quoting. Adequate protection to corners and sides from damage during the construction. Consolidation using vibrators of approved type . Work on all floors at levels as per drawing. Reinforcement steel is measured and paid under relevant item separately. Before concreting moisture content in the aggregates shall be found out and necessary corrections shall be applied as per Mix Design report. Rate to include bonding agents for bonding old concrete to new concrete wherever necessary. All Construction joints shall be at pre approved locations only. All Construction joints and concrete set surface shall be chipped immediately after final setting using mechanical chippers and thoroughly cleaned and shall be treated with epoxy bonding agent while concreting further without any extra cost. Expanded GI mesh shall be considered between the junction of difference in grade of concrete only in Column Drop, column drop & slab, column/core wall with shear walls. Roughening and hacking the concrete surfaces wherever required after removal of centring or shuttering. All tie rod holes shall be filled with approved grouting material. Forming key and construction joints, providing cement slurry work and cleaning at any stage of work, sand papering of existing reinforcement bars of column and dowels . before taking up fresh work. Coarse graded sand/ manufactured sand shall be used for all concrete works. Machine crushed coarse graded aggregate shall be hard broken blue granite of sizes specified. Cement for all concrete works shall only be High strength ordinary Portland cement Grade-53. Designing the concrete mix and batching s and approved concrete design mix with necessary admixtures for various workability and surface finish requirements. Adding of admixture to meet the required standards and specifications for various workability, consistency, self compaction, retardation, acceleration, surface finish, colour, uniformity, strength, shrinkage control, durability, fire rating, cohesiveness in the concrete as appropriate. Limiting the temperature of concrete and heat of hydration by adopting various temperature control measures like usage ice water, soaking of aggregates in water, storage in shaded areas, transportation and pumping with protective covers to meet the requirements. The rate shall include use of plasticizers and retarders to limit the water/cement ratio to 0.5 and the temperature to 35 degree C at the time of placing. care shall be taken to achieve dense and fully watertight concrete. Providing sunk floors, pockets, cut-outs, holes . Taking test cubes for all concrete items and testing them as specified in IS 456 at the site laboratory to be set up with proper equipment . and other regular test shall be carried out either at site Laboratory or at any approved test laboratory. Cost of in situ test on concrete like slump test, L-Box test, U box test, core test, ultrasonic test, rebound hammer test to prove the soundness of the element is included in rates and shall be carried out even though cube test results are passing the strength criteria as required by the consultants. Cost to include appropriate dewatering wherever necessary, cost of all , s, all lead and lifts, tools, plant and machinery, hire and fuel charges and all other incidental charges ., all s, drawings and . For all structural concrete elements post construction dimension and levels shall be documented and submitted. Concrete pour plan with construction joints, pour joints, shrinkage strips shall be submitted for approval. Shuttering below the shrinkage strip / pour joints at every level shall be retained in place without disturbing for a time as specified by the structural engineer. Any rectification of honeycombs not affecting structural stability, slurry leakage shall be rectified as per the methodology duly approved by client / the Engineer without any extra cost. All the material test certificates from the manufacturer and the external approved reputed testing laboratory, standard deviation and post concreting check results shall be submitted to the consultant. (Design mix shall be as approved by the Structural Consultant / Employers representative. Cementacious material GGBS can be used in the design mix with a proportion of 65:35 for grade concrete upto M35. GGBS 25% for grade concrete M40 and above. Raft concrete can be 50% of GGBS. Polycarboxylic Ether based admixures to be used for the Concrete of higher grade than M40 and Micro silica shall be used for the concrete of grade higher than M45) For Terrace Slab / Beam - No GGBS/Fly ash is allowed Manufactured sand can be considered . Core test shall be conducted on all elements for acceptance of strength of concrete achieved at site irrespective of the cube strength achieved as confirmatory test for the stability of the building. The total no. of core test shall generally be restricted to 50 Nos of the total concrete volume. Making all sunk floors, pockets, cutouts, holes, sleeves, embedding electrical conduits, junction boxes, fan hooks, cutout boxes and other inserts, sleeves hold fasts, pipes as specified in the drawings. All the lifting equipments tower crane, boom placer, Pumps, pumping lines, material hoist, lifts shall have independent supporting systems and shall not transfer any stress on building components. Minimum cementitious content for concrete shall be as per cement constants enclosed in the Technical specification. All Reinforced cement concrete works shall be done in accordance with technical specification . Providing & laying plant mixed design mix reinforced cement concrete conforming to grades as specified in (IS 456 - 2000) and s mentioned below at all levels using 20mm & down size aggregates, fine aggregates, OPC, necessary admixtures approved by Consultants (Conplast SP 430SRV from Fosroc or equivalent), designing the mix, trial cubes, accelerated curing, weigh batching, mechanical mixing, transporting, pumping and placing at all levels, excluding the cost of steel reinforcement, centring, shuttering but inclusive of cost towards pumping using line pump or boom placer, scaffolding, compacting with vibrators, construction joint treatments, curing and hacking the exposed surfaces wherever required to receive plaster or floated steel trowelled surface whenever specified. The rate shall include cost of arrangements to provide recesses to slabs, columns, walls and placing and securing properly within the formwork sleeves and / or opening in slabs, construction joints, laying to required shape, size and slope all as per drawings. The mix proportion of cement with that of GGBS shall be confirming to latest IS standards. The rate shall include providing and fixing the required PVC sleeves up to 200 mm dia pockets and opening ., cleaning the site debris stacking the material, hoisting equipment's, shutter accessories and fixing pipe / plates / inserts in perfect size level , plumb . by Engineer In Charge and as per technical specifications in the tender. Repairing / replacing the formwork, filling all the holes created due to shuttering / sleeves provided for passage of plumbing, FF pipes / Electrical cables with approved non shrink filler material . to the satisfaction of Client / the Engineer. Hacking of RCC faces as specified and directed by Engineer In Charge to be included in quoted rate of RCC. Cost to include appropriate dewatering wherever necessary, cost of all , s, all lead and lifts, tools, plant and machinery, hire and fuel charges and all other incidental charges ., all s, drawings and .

Detailed Description:
Providing, placing and compacting Smart Dynamic Cement Concrete / Free flow concrete, High flow concrete, non-segregate, free flow conforming to specified grade (IS 456-2000) the RMC of various grades with 20 mm and down graded aggregate of granite basalt or trap, in positions at all levels and heights, in all required shapes / profiles / inclinations for all reinforced concrete members such as RCC walls, columns, Shear walls, Core walls,  and other miscellaneous members and all architectural features as per specifications  / IS codes, including designing the mix, trial cubes, accelerated curing, weigh batching, mechanical mixing, transporting, pumping and placing at all levels, inclusive of cost towards pumping using line pump or boom placer, scaffolding, compacting with vibrators, construction joint treatments, curing and hacking the exposed surfaces wherever required to receive plaster or floated steel trowelled surface whenever specified. The rate shall include cost of  arrangements to provide recesses to slabs, columns, walls, Drip moulds,  and placing and securing properly within the formwork sleeves and / or opening in slabs, construction joints, laying to required shape, size and slope all as per drawings and as directed by the Engineer In Charge.
The rate shall also include the cost of necessary labour, plant and machineries, dewatering, curing the concrete as directed by the Engineer In Charge. curing pipes / Hessain cloth, tools and tackles, making the surface good to receive the finishes, Cover blocks of PVC of approved make / quality (in case of concrete cover blocks, One grade higher Ready made / Factory made to be used as against the relevant structural member grade), making provision of stand - by vibrators and needles , buttering with cement slurry , removing laitance from surface , protecting the newly  placed concrete from rain / heat with PVC sheets , marking the date of concreting, filling the required checklist / pour card, making necessary arrangements for ponding, curing, clearing the area of debris, providing required labour, pumping and hoisting by any mechanical means etc. complete as directed and to the satisfaction of the Client / the Engineer as per the technical specification of the tender.
The rate shall include providing and fixing the required PVC sleeves up to 200 mm dia pockets and opening etc., cleaning the site debris stacking the material, hoisting equipment's, shutter accessories and fixing pipe / plates / inserts in perfect size level, plumb etc. as directed by Client / the Engineer and as per technical specifications in the tender. Repairing / replacing the formwork, filling all the holes created due to shuttering / sleeves provided for passage of plumbing, FF pipes / Electrical cables with approved non shrink filler material etc. complete to the satisfaction of Client / the Engineer. Hacking of RCC faces as specified and directed by Client / the Engineer to be included in quoted rate of RCC.
The rates shall include the cost of necessary admixtures approved by consultant to get required workability & strength (Glenium series or equivalent) with flow dia of approximately 600 mm and sufficient retention in workability to avoid cold joints. use ordinary Portland cement of grade 53 from approved manufacturer & GGBS of approved brand in proportions of supplementary cementations materials as per Indian Standards. and follow the provident Standard Work Procedures for approval, Mix design, execution and quality measures.
Shear walls and Core Walls at all levels

Detailed Description:
Unless otherwise specified rates quoted for all Form works shall include the following :
Cost of all materials, labour, equipment / tools & plants required, lead and lifts, conveyance, infrastructure facilities required etc.
Form work shall include splayed edges, notching, allowances for overlaps and passing at angles, batterns, strutting, bolting, wedging, casing, rocking, centering, boxing, shuttering, propping strut braces etc., removal thereof and coating the shuttering with releasing agent /oil to prevent cement sticking to shutter faces. Form work shall be either straight, curved, plain or tapered and also with provision for specified slopes, if directed. Wind tie bolts for wall shuttering.
Cost of suitable framework and bracing according to the floor height specified in the drawing, moulds, centering and shuttering, single / double height scaffolding, as directed at site. Dimension of form work will be within the tolerance as specified in IS 456 and shuttering should be water tight.  Shuttering materials shall be Aluminum / steel / timber or plywood as suitable for the work intended.
The Contractor shall ensure that they have adequate quantity of shuttering and suitable propping materials according to the floor height specified in the drawing to keep up the required Concreting cycle per floor as set out in the Tender Documents, Drawings and Programme. They shall also ensure that the de-shuttering sequence with proper propping, taking into account the Construction Loading of the upper slabs, as set out in the Tender Drawings, is also kept up. Scheme to maintain the props shall ensure that main supports are not disturbed. Reproping by means of removal and reintroducing of props is not allowed.
Formwork for construction joints shall be submitted for approval. Shuttering below the shrinkage strip at every level shall be retained in place without disturbing for a time as specified by the structural engineer. Any honey coombs, any undulations, bulging, any leakage of slurry,  concrete etc is not acceptable and decision of the structural consultant along with the architect is final.
Formwork shall be designed for close jointed rigid, durable and shall have adequate strength, water tightness and ensure easy removal etc., Props, shorings and bracings shall be all in steel. Formwork designed with proposed materials (to be approved prior to making) shall be able to retain its shape, line, dimension, level within the allowable limits of variations. Necessary arrangement shall be envisaged to provide camber in beams or slabs as per design.
Contractor is responsible for the correctness and stability of the temporary works required for the peramanent works. All design parameters including design details, shop drawings, method statements and  material test certificates from external approved repuated testing laboratory shall be submitted to the consultant.
Exposed surfaces shall be free of blamish, residues of curing compound, uniform in colour, free of construction joint except detailed or approved under methodology of construction, forming construction joint or as providing joint free/ hole free water retaining structures. All system to have in built supporting safety screens.
All tie rod holes to be filled with approved grouting material. Cost to include necessary embedded tie rod for fixing the one sided shuttering and water retaining structures.
Shuttering & deshuttering sequence shall be 100%, 80% & 40%  from the slab to be cast respectively depending on the progress. The same is as per the details enumerated in General Notes. Contractors shall submit drawings showing the scheme of formwork including back propping and proper sequence of removal of formwork and back propping so that the structural slab will not be overloaded due to construction loads. All slabs shall be progressively deshuttered and back propped. It is compulsory to provide adequate back props to the slab on which the supports are resting before taking up concreting work for every slab.
Minimum of 150mm high MS frame works is required for kickers and starters in case required and formwork design calculations including re-propping arrangements needs to be submitted for approval.
Providing foam sheet at joints to prevent leakage of slurry during compaction, removing adhesive tape from concrete surface if any after deshuttering
All Form works shall be done in accordance with technical specification.

TYPE: SUBITEM

PARENT ITEM DESCRIPTION:
Unless otherwise specified rates quoted for all Form works shall include the following : Cost of all , , equipment / tools & plants required, lead and lifts, conveyance, infrastructure facilities required . Form work shall include splayed edges, notching, allowances for overlaps and passing at angles, batterns, strutting, bolting, wedging, casing, rocking, centering, boxing, shuttering, propping strut braces ., removal thereof and coating the shuttering with releasing agent /oil to prevent cement sticking to shutter faces. Form work shall be either straight, curved, plain or tapered and also with provision for specified slopes, if directed. Wind tie bolts for wall shuttering. Cost of suitable framework and bracing according to the floor height specified in the drawing, moulds, centering and shuttering, single / double height scaffolding, at site. Dimension of form work will be within the tolerance as specified in IS 456 and shuttering should be water tight. Shuttering shall be Aluminum / steel / timber or plywood as suitable for the work intended. The Contractor shall ensure that they have adequate quantity of shuttering and suitable propping according to the floor height specified in the drawing to keep up the required Concreting cycle per floor as set out in the Tender Documents, Drawings and Programme. They shall also ensure that the de-shuttering sequence with proper propping, taking into account the Construction Loading of the upper slabs, as set out in the Tender Drawings, is also kept up. Scheme to maintain the props shall ensure that main supports are not disturbed. Reproping by means of removal and reintroducing of props is not allowed. Formwork for construction joints shall be submitted for approval. Shuttering below the shrinkage strip at every level shall be retained in place without disturbing for a time as specified by the structural engineer. Any honey coombs, any undulations, bulging, any leakage of slurry, concrete is not acceptable and decision of the structural consultant along with the architect is final. Formwork shall be designed for close jointed rigid, durable and shall have adequate strength, water tightness and ensure easy removal ., Props, shorings and bracings shall be all in steel. Formwork designed with proposed (to be approved prior to making) shall be able to retain its shape, line, dimension, level within the allowable limits of variations. Necessary arrangement shall be envisaged to provide camber in beams or slabs as per design. Contractor is responsible for the correctness and stability of the temporary works required for the peramanent works. All design parameters design details, shop drawings, method statements and material test certificates from external approved repuated testing laboratory shall be submitted to the consultant. Exposed surfaces shall be free of blamish, residues of curing compound, uniform in colour, free of construction joint except detailed or approved under methodology of construction, forming construction joint or as providing joint free/ hole free water retaining structures. All system to have in built supporting safety screens. All tie rod holes to be filled with approved grouting material. Cost to include necessary embedded tie rod for fixing the one sided shuttering and water retaining structures. Shuttering & deshuttering sequence shall be 100%, 80% & 40% from the slab to be cast respectively depending on the progress. The same is as per the details enumerated in General Notes. Contractors shall submit drawings showing the scheme of formwork back propping and proper sequence of removal of formwork and back propping so that the structural slab will not be overloaded due to construction loads. All slabs shall be progressively deshuttered and back propped. It is compulsory to provide adequate back props to the slab on which the supports are resting before taking up concreting work for every slab. Minimum of 150mm high MS frame works is required for kickers and starters in case required and formwork design calculations re-propping arrangements needs to be submitted for approval. Providing foam sheet at joints to prevent leakage of slurry during compaction, removing adhesive tape from concrete surface if any after deshuttering All Form works shall be done in accordance with technical specification.

Detailed Description:
Providing and fixing the Aluminium shuttering of  'MIVAN' or equivalent  in the form of an assembled formwork for the monolithic casting of the walls, beams, slabs, folded slabs, staircases, openings, shafts, pardis, sunshades, projected slabs, balconies, window boxing, sleeves, etc, with all elements of the mould assembled as per the approved design and as per the manufacturer's specifications. Only the surface area of the form work to be measured. The work includes the survey and proper setting out of formwork while assembling and re-assembling on floors, Initial setting,  providing necessary starters, application of approved make and grade of shuttering oil, provision for dowels for construction joint, providing and fixing steel props, bracings, strutts, ties with PVC sleeves; providing staging/supports for slabs and fixing of working platform required all around the external walls at two levels; Fixing of Safety nets for the full height of two floors at working levels, Removal of formwork after concreting, propping of the slab for the required periods, hacking wherever required, cleaning the formwork and making good the whole, rectification of damages and replacement of defective formwork or accessories, cost of all consumables like Pin & Wedge, Patti, Grooves, foam sheets etc, maliting of formwork for proper surface finish, shifting of formwork vertically as well as horizontally for further assemblies etc. The rate shall including  for double height balconies, which are flipping in alternate floors with additional staging for concreting for slab/beams/coumns and finishing the same. Rate shall include mobilisation of staging material, erecting as and when the construction progresses, making Drip moulds, elevation features etc, keeping the same till final completion of related works and deshuttering/demobilising the same after approval from the Client / the Engineer
The rate shall also include for designing and submitting of formwork drawings for approvals, providing and applying approved moulds released agent, de-shuttering / removing the formwork. The concrete joint marks shall be smoothed off and all blemishes, projections etc. removed, leaving the surfaces reasonably smooth and unmarred by any means to receive Internal/External plaster / Internal/External Paint as the case may be. Providing necessary stopper at construction joints, platform for concreting, cut-outs in the formwork, providing and fixing the required sleeves, pockets and opening , MS Jali etc. cleaning the site debris, stacking the material, hosting, equipment's, and fixing pipe /plate/inserts in perfect line, level, plumb with due protection against any damage or blockage etc. as directed and as per technical specification in the tender by the Client / the Engineer. repairing / replacing the formwork, filling all the holes created due to shuttering with approved filler material etc. complete to the satisfaction of the Client / the Engineer.

Work at any depth below ground level or any height above ground level and at all levels, heights & elevation and location. Final preparation of base, sub-grade or sub-floor by cleaning of all dust/dirt, loose particles, caulked mortar dropping by scrubbing with coir or steel wire brushes, caulking . Roughening surface if so directed, cleaning with water and keeping surface wet for 12 hours and removing surplus water by mopping before topping is laid and minor trimming of the base to remove undulations. Cleaning, preparation of surface and watering immediately before laying the floor . Keeping the surface wet for a minimum period of one week. Tests on shall conform to IS Specification and the test shall be carried out either at site Laboratory or at any approved test laboratory. VDF operation shall be carried out . Joints/Grooves shall be done within 48hours after the slab has been casted at specified width and depth, sealing the grooves with joint sealants as specified. Designing the concrete mix and batching as per the required specifications using neseccary cementisious , shrinkage compensating admixture, fibres . to achive the desired finish and crack free flooring is included in the rates.

Detailed Description:
NOTE: 1.Specialized agency to be approved by Consultants/Client
1. The guarantee for waterproofing should be provided by the principal company for 10years for both material and application.
2.Guarantee/warranty from applicators will not be acceptable. An undertaking in this regard from the principal company should be enclosed along with the letter for approval of waterproofing agency.
3.Testing of the surfaces prior to start the work by ponding, grouting or sealing of joints, making watta at corners/ junctions etc are deemed to be included for all items while quoting rates. If any leakage is found subsequently, it has to be made water tight by the contractor free of cost. 
4.During construction, all the construction joints of water bodies should be provided with butyl rubber swellable bars at all construction joints, retaining walls joints both from horizontal and Vertical joints using standard manufacturers like BASF/SIKA/FOSROC or equivalent as approved by the Client / the Engineer.
5. It is the responsibility of the contractor to maintain safe and clean working conditions in all activities related to the water proofing works at all time.
6. Utmost care should be taken to prevent any accident during the waterproofing work as some works are carried out in confined areas and dealing with chemicals.
7. Those who are working should have personal protective equipments PPE's like hand gloves, goggles, shoe, Helmet, mask,etc.,
8.   Manufacturer's specifications should be strictly  followed to avoid any accidents due to improper handling of materials.
9. Materials should be stored as per manufacturer specifications.
10. Materials  should be stored with proper labelling to avoid improper usage of consumption of any material by any workers.
11. Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) should be available at the time of delivery  of materials.
12. Adequate  fire  protection  measures  should  be  available  at  storage  and application location as some of the products are combustible.
13. Risk assessment should be carried out before the work starts. Risk assessment should  contain all  possible  risks, risk  rating without  control  measures, control measures and risk rating after implementation of corrective measures. Tool box talks should be conducted  every week explaining possible risks and its control measures. .
14. Clean environment should be maintained all around the work place.
15. All vessels and tools used for mixing and application of chemicals should be cleaned using appropriate cleaning materials suggested by the manufacturers. 
16. Water and balance materials after cleaning should be disposed off in such a way that it is not polluting the environment
17. Approved  method statement and material details should be available before starting the work.
18. Waterproofing  work  should  be carried  out  on  concrete  surface  which  are minimum 28 days old.
19. Waterproofing shall be carried out on sound concrete surfaces only.
20. Manufacturer specification  for  handling, mixing, application and storage  of material should be strictly followed.
21. Water  pond  test  should  be  conducted  after  surface  preparation  and  completion of waterproofing work to ensure water tightness.
22. All materials used to be supported with third party certificates / external laboratory test report as and when insisted by the Client / the Engineer specifically while start of work or on need basis, the same cost is with the scope of concerned water proofing agency.

1. Surface preparation: Before starting waterproofing works the surface of the wall should be made free of any loose particles, vegetation, grease or oily matters . All tie rod holes should be filled properly. All honeycombs should be repaired as per approved repair procedures. Treating the Construction Joints: Chase opening the Construction Joints by providing ''V " groove, removing the dust and clean the designated area following by providing with approved nonshrink high strength cemenitious grout, finishing, curing . as per manufacturers specification and .Any damp spots on the walls should be attended by grouting cement mixed with flow enhancers like Cebex-100 or equivalent until the leak or dampness is fully arrested. When PVC pipes are inserted in the designated openings in the slab, it should be applied with epoxy bonding agent sprayed with sand on the portion coming in contact with non shrink grout or mortar or concrete to enhance the bonding and packed with non shrink grout scafolding . All sharp edges and corners to be chamferred / haunched to receive the membrane. 2. Providing and applying Two coats of cold applied, flexible, damp proof bituminous emulsion coating with approx. density of 1.0kg/litre @+250C, Minimum 10% rubber content and a coverage yield of minimum 0.5 litre/Sqm .The coating shall be laid over a compatible primer as per Manufacturer's specification with sufficient overlaps. 3. This membrane should be protected with 250 GSM minimum non woven polyester mat fixed as per manufacturer specifications. 4. The emulsion coating and he protection layer should have sufficient overlap as per manufacturer specifications. 5. The applicator/manufacturer should submit the details of waterproofing near the penetrations on the wall. 6. The membrane should be terminated on all sides properly with watertight joints. 7.The manufacturer/applicator should submit the details and get client approval prior to start the work. 8.The contractor to include cost of termination of waterproofing system with necessary overlapping, joint treatment, all as per manufacturer's recommendations.The rate shall include all leads,lifts staging/scaffolding, required for the application. 9.Treatment shall be provided from standard manufacturers like BASF, SIKA, FOSROC or equivalent. Providing waterproofing to the roof by the following method with 10 years guarantee, which consists of : 9.Sand sprinkle to be given for vertical wall surface over the PU waterproof coating to make proper mechanical key for bonding the plaster surface using special bonding agents . Any damp spots on the slab should be attended by grouting cement mixed with flow enhancers like Cebex-100 or equivalent until the leak or dampness is arrested. 2.After treating the surface as mentioned above apply liquid applied, high performance, pure polyurethane based, single component, low VOC, elastomeric, seamless, moisture curing, waterproof membrane, applied in 2 coats with roller / airless spray,(As required by the manufacturer specifications but minimum thickness (DFT) shall pass 1.5 mm) with over 400% elongation and sufficient puncture resistance and anti-root penetration capacity over one coat of compatible primer at a coverage as per the manufacturer's recommendation . The spray applied membrane should be seamless, free of any holes. Overlap should be as per manufacturer specifications. 3. When spray applied or brush coated procedure is adopted, the coverage of the material should be noted on a daily basis and to be submitted to client. 4.At the expansion joints and termination, the detailing for the membrane application shall be submitted to the Client / the Engineer/QA approval prior to commencing the work. 5.The PU waterproofing membrane should be extended 300mm above FFL on the vertical surface and tucked into the wall by making a groove. 6.Ponding tests for 72 hours should be conducted after the completion of application of membrane to ensure water tightness. 7.Treatment shall be provided from standard manufacturers like FOSROC,BASF,SIKA / Equivalent. 8.Thereafter 250 GSM non woven polypropylene geotextile for horizontal surface., For Tiling Area: For Floor: Providing & Laying average 75mm THK protective screed of M15 grade with slope 1:120 or as per approved GFC containing 12 mm downsize aggregates, 100% virgin polypropylene fibers @ 0.9 Kg per Cu.m well compacted, to be broom finish to receive tiling, curing for 7 days, Screed to be laid in panels of 4m wide not more than 200sqm/or as per site condition using alternate panel method and the control joints shall be provided for every 16 sqm, 10mm X 100mm thermocol to be placed at the peripheral corners prior to laying screed and later 10 mm wide X 30 mm deep thermocol to be removed from screed top and to be filled with ShaliTex Bitumen Based Sealing Compound or Equivalent. Construction joint treatment to be ensured in the Screed panels . . NOTE: Only Plan areas shall be considered and necessary shop drawings shall be submitted by contractor for consultants/client approval prior to execution., For vertical surface : 12mm thick protective plaster on the wall in cement mortar (1:4), mixing integral waterproofing compound as per manufacturer specifications bonding agent., For Non Tiling Area (Screed concrete as final finish): For Floor: Providing & Laying average 75mm THK protective screed of M15 grade with slope 1:150 or 1:100 as per approved GFC containing 12 mm downsize aggregates, 100% virgin polypropylene fibers @ 0.9 Kg per Cu.m with a smooth finish, well compacted, curing for 7 days . . 10mm X 100mm thermocol to be placed at the peripheral corners prior to laying screed and later 10 mm wide X 30 mm deep thermocol to be removed from screed top and to be filled with ShaliTex Bitumen Based Sealing Compound or Equivalent. The screed shall be finished with checkered marking of 600 mm x 600mm using 3 mm thread and panels of 3000mm X 3000mm shall be formed by saw cut groove of size 6 mm wide X 30 mm deep (with in 20 hrs time) which then filled with ShaliTex Bitumen Based Sealing Compound by covering either sides neatly with masking tape to avoid spillages, ., clean and . thick protective plaster on the wall in cement mortar (1:4), mixing integral waterproofing compound as per manufacturer specifications bonding agent., Providing & Laying clay Tiles as per IS 1478:1992 of approved size and thickness, of 1st class quality in approved colors/shades to floor in required position set on bed of cement mortar 1:4 (1 Cement : 4 Manufactured Sand) of minimum 30mm thk to required slopes as per drawing cement slurry matching the staggered design patterns, Joints to run horizontally and vertically with 10 mm spacers, levelling, lining, grouting the joints with greycement mxed grouts or equivalent grouting prigments added to match the colour of the tile or contrast to tile s from drawings, rubbing, washing, curing, cleaning and protecting finished surface from scratches, damage,stains and other damages at level with POP over bubbled polythene sheet or equivalent ., ., Providing & Laying sloped clay Tile skirting of approved size and thickness, per IS 1478:1992, of 1st class quality in approved colors/shades to floor in required position set on bed of cement mortar 1:4 (1 Cement : 4 Manufactured Sand) of minimum 30mm thk to required slopes as per
